methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Fortera is a Series C climate and energy company that has raised $200M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private companies (KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy), alongside a broader comparable universe of 9 private firms and 1 public company in the climate tech space.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $340M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the median valuation across the three direct comparables at $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M (Amogy) to $2.0B (KoBold Metals). The peer-median method reflects market pricing for functionally similar stage and sector positioning.

Triangulating both approaches produces a valuation range of $289M to $1.1B, with a median of $670M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in the peer set—KoBold's $2.0B valuation substantially exceeds Amogy's $700M despite identical staging—suggesting technology differentiation, market opportunity size, or capital efficiency perception drives pricing heterogeneity within this cohort. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, capital efficiency trajectory through Series D, and the degree to which Fortera's specific technology or market addresses a premium or discount relative to the observable range.
